task_completion.description: Navigate to the OpenMRS Administration login page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open to https://localhost/openmrs/admin (or similar OpenMRS path) showing the OpenMRS login screen.

task_completion.description: Log into the OpenMRS Administration panel using the provided credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenMRS Administration dashboard/panel is visible, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Privilege management section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Manage Privileges' or 'Privileges' list page is displayed in the OpenMRS interface.

task_completion.description: Enter the new privilege name and description in the Add Privilege form.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Add Privilege' form is visible and filled out with the name 'View Triage Queue' and a description containing the words 'triage' and 'queue'.

task_completion.description: Save the new privilege so it is added to the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message indicating the privilege was saved, or the privilege 'View Triage Queue' is clearly visible as an entry in the list of existing privileges.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the OpenMRS GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: A standard web browser (e.g., Chrome, Firefox, Chromium) is visible in the screenshots being used to navigate the OpenMRS application.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands (like curl or mysql) to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent using terminal commands, API scripts, or direct database queries to create the privilege.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine OpenMRS web application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown is the actual OpenMRS web UI, not a text editor, mock HTML file, or notes app.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected privilege name to be created is exactly 'View Triage Queue'. The description must contain the keywords 'triage' and 'queue' (e.g., 'Allows users to view the patient triage queue and priority levels'). The agent should use the credentials username: 'superman' and password: 'Admin123' to log into the OpenMRS Administration panel at https://localhost/openmrs/admin.
